title: KI ist keine Zahnpasta | Funding-Woche: Databricks, Waymo, OpenAI, Lovable & Yann LeCuns AMI Labs #520
description: Die Woche der Mega-Funding-Runden: Databricks wird mit 134 Milliarden bewertet, Waymo peilt 100 Milliarden an, und OpenAI soll bald 830 Milliarden wert sein. Amazon will 10 Milliarden in OpenAI investieren. Coinbase launcht Prediction Markets und tokenisierte Aktien. Revolut plant 3,5 Milliarden Profit bei 40% Marge – profitabler als die meisten Banken. Lovable aus Schweden rast auf 6,6 Milliarden. Das US-Handelsministerium droht der EU mit Vergeltung wegen Tech-Regulierung und nennt explizit SA

title: OpenAI's ChatGPT Pulse Debuts, Musk's AI Strategy, and Spotify's Crackdown on Spam Tracks
description:
Welcome to the AI Pulse Podcast, where we dive into the latest developments in artificial intelligence and its impact on our world. Here are the top stories for today:

OpenAI is launching ChatGPT Pulse, a new feature that offers personalized morning briefs to replace the morning scroll through social media or news apps. Currently available for Pro users, OpenAI aims to expand access as server capacity grows.

In the realm of AI hype, figures like Sam Altman are likening AI's capabilities to mag

title: Inside OpenAI’s $500B Valuation | Altimeter’s Largest Bet
description:
With $10B+ in AUM, Altimeter has made their largest bet in its history on OpenAI. Known for disciplined bets in generational companies like Snowflake, SpaceX, Anduril — and now OpenAI, the firm is now fully committed to the AI supercycle.

In this episode, Apoorv Agrawal, Partner at Altimeter focused on AI and software, joins Molly O’Shea to break down how the firm evaluates opportunities in the AI supercycle. Apoorv explains why power laws govern venture returns, how Altimeter evaluated the $50

title: Data + Women Netherlands 05 Mar 2026
description: Ready for a Women’s Day event that skips the fluff? Join us for a look at the tech industry’s evolution. We’re kicking things off with an opening keynote by Eva Fors (Databricks, ex-Google & Microsoft). With 30 years in the field—starting at Microsoft in '95 and leading through the rise of Google and Databricks—Eva has seen the industry’s transformation firsthand. She’ll be reflecting on the reality of those changes: while we’ve moved from physical server rooms to the era of GenAI, has social pr

title: Decoding Musk's Personnel Strategy: AI Economy Workforce Insights and Career Trajectories
description: Dive deep into the evolving strategies guiding technological leadership in this comprehensive update focusing on Elon Musk's latest directives. This episode explores the seismic shifts in his approach to talent acquisition, moving away from traditional academic pedigree toward demonstrable conversational substance—a crucial insight for anyone aiming for roles at SpaceX or within the cutting-edge Optimus robot projects. We analyze the current AI economy landscape, contrasting the speculative hype

title: AI Ads Hit the NBA Finals | Kalshi, Databricks, Luma, and the Burnout Era of AI
description:
AI just crashed primetime. Kalshi dropped a fully AI-generated ad during the NBA Finals — no cameras, no crew, just Google’s Veo model and a national TV slot.
We talk to Kalshi’s Head of Growth on how it happened, Luma AI’s CEO on what’s coming next for generative video, and Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi on why Big Tech’s AI teams are burning out.
Plus, Jeff Richards of Notable Capital joins to unpack what it all means for investors.

title: 346: Zuckerberg Finally Finds His People, They Are All AI Agents
show: The Cloud Pod | Weekly AI & Cloud News on AWS, Azure & GCP
description:
Welcome to episode 346 of The Cloud Pod, where the forecast is always cloudy! Hold on to your butts, because Justin, Ryan, and Matt are in the studio today, and they’re ready to bring you all the latest in Cloud and AI news, including the usual: Meta buying social networks, Amazon responding to outages, and OpenAI giving up another version of GPT. Let’s get into it! 

Titles we almost went with this week

✍️ Cloudflare Spent $1100 to Rewrite Next.js in a Week
 One Pipe to Rule All Your OpenTelem

title: Unlocking Value with Ari Kaplan from Databricks
description: In this episode I sat down with Ari Kaplan Head of Evangelism at Databricks. We talk about how organizations are transforming their data and AI strategies with AI-powered intelligence platforms. We explore the evolution from traditional analytics to democratized AI insights, the critical role of data governance and lineage in AI implementations, and how generative AI is revolutionizing everything from data engineering workflows to application development. We also discuss the real-world impact of

title: Snowflake & Databricks Cross the Rubicon into a New Competitive Domain
A16Z's David George on How Private and Public Markets Fused Into One
description: This year could be a big one for IPOs. From Anthropic to SpaceX to OpenAI, we could see some gigantic companies hit the public market. But of course, the big story is that big, thriving companies feel less and less pressure to go public. In a different era, private giants like Databricks and Stripe might've IPO'd a long time ago. So what's changed? Why are companies comfortable staying private for so long? On this episode, we speak with David George, a general partner at Andreesen Horowitz, who

title: Hardware's brutal week: iRobot, Luminar, and Rad Power go bankrupt
description:
The hardware world had a brutal week, with iRobot, Luminar, and Rad Power Bikes all filing for bankruptcy. 

Each company faces its own mix of tariff pressures, supply chain issues, and shifting markets, but together they tell a larger story about the challenges of building physical products in an era of global trade tensions and cheap overseas competition. From the Roomba maker that almost got acquired by Amazon to the e-bike company that couldn't escape its Chinese supply chain, this week's ba

title: Does Anthropic Owe Me $3000?
description:
Anthropic has agreed to pay $1.5 billion dollars to authors of books that might have trained their AI. Would my book qualify for some of that money? Also, why this is interesting in general. OpenAI is making a movie. Tokenizing the stock market. Three interesting raises, and at the end of the show, let me tell you about my weekend experiment with AI.

title: Databricks IPO and the Next Three Years in AI
show: Investing in Intelligence: AI Stocks and Options
description: Investing in AI stocks in 2025 is equally gratifying and terrifying with the ever-present threat of tariffs and their possible economic impacts. We break down what really matters most in economic indicators - since the overall economy affects AI stocks more than anything - before we take a look at what the next three years in AI will bring. Learning from the successes of Circle and CoreWeave - we tip you off to the next big AI IPO (DataBricks). DataBricks is expected to IPO before the conclusion
